candidates.evidence_quote: I know in 2024, the stable labor market has been a huge tailwind for you.
As Matt stated earlier, these are all-time lows in attrition for the company.
You did mention the CSAT scores are record high
And as we talked about earlier, this new menu has a great range of price points, everything from $12.95 to $31.
First, I wanted to ask about North Italia comps. I just want to make sure I can contextualize them, given that for the last several quarters, growth has outpaced core cheesecake pretty meaningfully.
So the traffic for North was very similar to Cheesecake Factory, just slightly negative.
The mix was a little bit of a heavier impact for North. It has been kind of for the past two to three quarters. And really, again, that's in the alcohol category.
it takes a few years for AUVs to build up so that you can leverage your costs
So when we go into existing markets for both North and Flower, we see the sales ramp up faster because of the brand awareness.
acknowledged that they're seeing an increasingly anxious consumer in recent weeks
I mean, the weather impacts have been very, very clear to us.
implying that there's some heightened price sensitivities out there
We never get into the discounting wars, right?
Well, regarding, say, attachments rates, if you will, we're still above 2019 levels.
Right now, we do anticipate one closure with Cheesecake Factory.
So we would have the 25 new, and we'd have one closure around the middle of the year for Cheesecake.
And that's included in the revenue outlook.
really impressive cheesecake for all margins. I think you mentioned 18.4% this quarter, highest in over seven years
The mature margin today is 18.8, quite a nice improvement, especially relative to, you know, comps that were up about 1%.
So for the full year, Flower Child and Mature were 17%, same as Cheesecake Factory.
So certainly North Italia plays well in the holiday season and special occasion and drove really strong volumes at the mature level.
To confirm, do you expect to be in that 1% to 2% range on comp in the first quarter?
So I think there's some room on both COGS and labor, although not as much, right?
For Q, it looks like you had very good favorability on the labor line.
The cost of utilities has gone up a little bit faster possibly
certainly the pre-opening costs that we talked about are the other kind of key components there
And on the commodities, it continues to be, you know, fairly benign outside of eggs, of course.
fairly benign outside of eggs, of course. I mean, that's a huge flashpoint for everybody, and we're watching that
We did actually have one culinary dropout in Atlanta that we did have to close and impair.
I wanted to dig in a little more on the flower child. What would be the potential average volumes for a fully mature box
Sure, it's about 50-50, roughly speaking. So we're about 50% on-premise, 50% off-premise.
